messages.author: Adrian Wik
messages.text: Hello, I need ethernet support for my stacionary companion (RAK with PoE on full RAK stuff). On Meshtastic this stuff works very well.
messages.content_html: Hello, I need ethernet support for my stacionary companion (RAK with PoE on full RAK stuff). On Meshtastic this stuff works very well.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: There is a pull request to add this in github already, but it hasn't been merged into mainline. If you can build it yourself, you could help test it and give feedback to help it make it into mainline.
messages.content_html: There is a pull request to add this in github already, but it hasn't been merged into mainline. If you can build it yourself, you could help test it and give feedback to help it make it into mainline.

messages.author: Adrian Wik
messages.content_html: Thank you

messages.author: Vaizki
messages.text: I have been running that branch for 3+ weeks without issues but it is a REPEATER firmware, not companion. Also Ethernet support is quite thin in that you can get to the console and issue commands (which is great) but firmware updates are still over BLE direct to the MCU and it can't act as an independent observer sending packets to MQTT without a separate PC or RasPi.
messages.content_html: I have been running that branch for 3+ weeks without issues but it is a REPEATER firmware, not companion. Also Ethernet support is quite thin in that you can get to the console and issue commands (which is great) but firmware updates are still over BLE direct to the MCU and it can't act as an independent observer sending packets to MQTT without a separate PC or RasPi.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: There is a native MQTT forwarder firmware, but it's for ESP32, not nRF. https://github.com/agessaman/MeshCore/tree/mqtt-bridge-implementation
messages.content_html: There is a native MQTT forwarder firmware, but it's for ESP32, not nRF. https://github.com/agessaman/MeshCore/tree/mqtt-bridge-implementation

messages.author: Vaizki
messages.text: Yes and we use it on most of the ESP32 nodes
messages.content_html: Yes and we use it on most of the ESP32 nodes
